summary: Full E2E testing of ATS Resume Scanner. All 11 backend pytest tests passed (health, auth, parse, scan, history, downloads, delete). Frontend dashboard upload/scan flow, results page, and history navigation all work via seeded session cookie. Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations produced ATS score 68 -> 94 on the sample inputs (meets >=85 requirement).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py stores expires_at as native datetime in create_session but as 7d-future datetime; auth helper handles both string and datetime which is fine.
PDF download endpoint requires auth via cookie/bearer - works but browser link-clicks won't carry Bearer; current cookie-based session works for in-app downloads.
Scan pipeline runs 3 LLM calls sequentially (~30s). Consider parallelizing analyze+optimize or streaming progress to UI for better UX.
test_credentials: Seeded mongosh user session_token=test_session_1737000000 (user_id=test-user-1737000000, email=test.user@example.com, name=Jane Doe). Use as Bearer token or session_token cookie.
seed_data_creation: Inserted one user + session in test_database via mongosh per /app/auth_testing.md.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Re-seed session via /app/auth_testing.md before re-running. Scan is slow (~30s) due to 3 sequential LLM calls; pytest module-scoped fixture caches the scan result to keep total runtime <30s.
